title: Linear and binary search

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Linear and binary search; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Linear and binary search
sources.claim: Linear search needs no ordering, while binary search maintains a sorted search interval and discards half after each comparison.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Linear search needs no ordering, while binary search maintains a sorted search interval and discards half after each comparison.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: lower_bound searches [lo,hi) for the first position whose value is not less than target. Your goal is to implement, trace and test linear and binary search including empty and duplicate cases. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Arrays, strings and matrices; Time, space and asymptotic growth; Array representation and operations; Correctness and counterexamples. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Linear search needs no ordering, while binary search maintains a sorted search interval and discards half after each comparison. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, pass buffers and counts and use qsort/bsearch comparators carefully; in C++, prefer standard algorithms and strict comparators; in Java, use Arrays/Collections algorithms with comparator contracts; in Python, use sorted/list.sort and bisect with key and stability semantics.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Binary search needs monotonic structure, careful half-open bounds, and overflow-safe midpoint calculation. The draft therefore rejects: Binary search works on any unsorted array if the midpoint is updated correctly.

includes: Search unsorted or sorted ranges with correct invariants and boundary updates.
Implement, trace and test linear and binary search including empty and duplicate cases.

In C, pass buffers and counts and use qsort/bsearch comparators carefully; in C++, prefer standard algorithms and strict comparators; in Java, use Arrays/Collections algorithms with comparator contracts; in Python, use sorted/list.sort and bisect with key and stability semantics.
